verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonical cytosolic Hsp70 chaperone with no intrinsic membrane anchor (TM, GPI, or outer-leaflet lipidation). However, it has well-documented non-baseline surface display in two key contexts: (1) cancer cells present Hsp70 on the outer leaflet as an immunogenic tumor-associated antigen, driving CAR-T and therapeutic antibody programs targeting surface-displayed Hsp70; (2) cell-death conditions (apoptosis, necroptosis, immunogenic cell death) externalize Hsp70 to the outer face of dying cells, where it serves as a damage-associated molecular pattern (DAMP) recognized by pattern-recognition receptors. Additionally, minor intracellular pools of Hsp70 may traffic to the PM via exocytosis during stress or activation. The baseline localization is cytosolic / ER-lumenal, but the documented cancer-ecto and cell-death-ecto pools constitute real surface-accessibility contexts.
